title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
summary: Jack Roberts describes Codex's /goal command, which lets developers skip step-by-step instructions and describe desired outcomes in plain English. Codex then enters the Ralph loop, autonomously planning, executing, testing, and self-correcting until the code works, presented as a hands-off alternative to traditional AI-assisted coding.
writeup: The video walks through Codex's /goal command, which lets developers state a desired outcome in plain English rather than supplying step-by-step instructions. From that goal, Codex enters what is called the Ralph loop, autonomously planning, executing, testing, and self-correcting until the code works. The framing matters because it shifts AI-assisted coding from guided prompting toward a hands-off agentic loop. The takeaway: outcome-level specification plus an autonomous test-and-correct cycle is positioned as an alternative to traditional step-by-step AI coding.
